name: Itty Critters
teaser: Summon small minions directly from your deck just for their effects and return them to the bottom of your deck at the end of the turn.
mechanics: Summon small minions directly from your deck just for their effects and return them to the bottom of your deck at the end of the turn.
description: Meet the Itty Critters, a fascinating faction from the Big in Japan expansion. By gaining the loyalty of special power-2 minions, you'll be equipped to tackle any challenge and unleash a surge of strength to claim victory. This faction is classified as having a High complexity rating. The Big in Japan set also features three other unique factions: Kaiju, Magical Girls, and Mega Troopers.
cardsTeaser:
The Itty Critters faction is composed of 10 minions and 10 actions, with a notable inclusion of a titan. Their minion base power totals 29, averaging 2.9 power per minion, slightly below the typical 30 total power and 3 power average.

Their action lineup features no play-on-minion actions, but includes two play-on-base actions, with Ittypedia appearing twice. The faction also boasts eight standard actions, including six that impact one or more minions: **Coach Combat**, **Critter Cube**, **Evolution** (twice), **Gotta Get ‘Em All**, **I Select You!**, **Recall Critter**, and **Super Effective!**. Notably, none of the Itty Critters' actions directly enhance a minion's power.
actionList:
1x Coach Combat - Choose one of your minions. Destroy a minion with lower power at its base.
1x Critter Cube - Choose a minion in play of power 3 or less, owned by any player, and shuffle it into your deck.
2x Evolution - Choose one of your minions. Destroy it to either play Rainboroc there, or search your deck for a minion with up to 1 more power and play it there as an extra minion.
1x Gotta Get ‘Em All - Shuffle one of each minion with a different name from your discard pile into your deck.
1x I Select You! - Search your deck for a minion of power 3 or less and play it as an extra minion. If you control it at the end the turn, place it on the bottom of your deck.
2x Ittypedia - Play on a base. Ongoing: After you play a minion here, it gains +1 power until the end of the turn.
1x Recall Critter - Play a minion of power 2 or less from your discard pile as an extra minion. If you control it at the end of the turn, place it on the bottom of your deck.
1x Super Effective! - Destroy an action on a minion or on a base.
actions:
1x Coach Combat - Choose one of your minions. Destroy a minion with lower power at its base.

1x Critter Cube - Choose a minion in play of power 3 or less, owned by any player, and shuffle it into your deck.

2x Evolution - Choose one of your minions. Destroy it to either play Rainboroc there, or search your deck for a minion with up to 1 more power and play it there as an extra minion.

1x Gotta Get ‘Em All - Shuffle one of each minion with a different name from your discard pile into your deck.

1x I Select You! - Search your deck for a minion of power 3 or less and play it as an extra minion. If you control it at the end the turn, place it on the bottom of your deck.

2x Ittypedia - Play on a base. Ongoing: After you play a minion here, it gains +1 power until the end of the turn.

1x Recall Critter - Play a minion of power 2 or less from your discard pile as an extra minion. If you control it at the end of the turn, place it on the bottom of your deck.

1x Super Effective! - Destroy an action on a minion or on a base.
characters:
1x Critter Champion - power 5 - Talent: Search your deck for a minion of power 2 or less and play it here as an extra minion. If you control it at the end of the turn, place it on the bottom of your deck.

3x Critter Coach - power 4 - You may search your deck for a minion of power 2 or less and play it here as an extra minion. If you control it at the end of the turn, place it on the bottom of your deck.

1x Calicoin - power 2 - You may place +1 power counter on another minion here.

1x Flooffairy - power 2 - You may draw a card.

1x Krakatoad - power 2 - You may choose another minion here to gain +2 power until the end of the turn.

1x Leafaroo - power 2 - You may shuffle a card from your discard pile into your deck.

1x Shellshock - power 2 - You may destroy another minion of power 2 or less here.

1x Tadpour - power 2 - You may move another minion from here to another base.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Big in Japan rulebook:

Critter Champion, Critter Coach, Evolution, I Select You: It is now part of the rules that cards that tell you to search your deck also require that you reveal the card searched for, and shuffle your deck afterwards.

Critter Coach, Critter Champion: The minion they play must go to the bottom of the deck even if the card that summoned them is out of play by the end of the turn.

Critter Cube: The other players’ minion in your deck is drawn and played normally, and you control it. When it leaves play it goes back to the owner.
suggestionTeaser: The Itty Critters thrive when paired with factions that can unleash a sudden surge of power and those that bring low-to-moderate-strength minions to the table, particularly ones with a power of 2 or less.
synergy:
Itty Critters thrive when paired with factions that can unleash a surge of power and those that boast minions with a power of 2 or less. 

Dinosaurs make for a formidable team, as Itty Critters can ambush bases with War Raptors and amplify their minions' strength with Rampage and other actions.

Minions of Cthulhu also complement Itty Critters well, allowing for a potent loop of abilities with Critter Champion, Altar of Cthulhu, Servitor of Cthulhu, and Rainboroc to replay powerful actions like Critter Cube or The Seal is Broken every turn. Additionally, Star-Spawn can be used to mitigate Madness, while Itty Critters enable the replaying of top minions.

Shapeshifters provide a valuable alliance, helping to track down specific minions such as Mimics and Copycats, ensuring that Itty Critters have the right tools at their disposal.
effects: At the heart of the Itty Critters faction are six distinct, adorable minions, each with a power value of 2 and a one-of-a-kind ability that triggers when played. These tiny powerhouses are backed by a range of supporting cards that help manage, sustain, and enhance their performance. The Itty Critters' strength lies in their versatility, allowing players to unleash brief yet potent bursts of power. This faction is also home to Rainboroc, a legendary titan and the oversized counterpart to the otherwise petite critters, as part of the Big in Japan expansion.

name: Kaiju
teaser: Play with fewer minions than normal, but gain power through your play-on-base actions, destroy other players' cards, manipulate bases and summon a giant freaking titan.
mechanics: Play with fewer minions than normal, but gain power through your play-on-base actions, destroy other players' cards, manipulate bases and summon a giant freaking titan.
description: In the Big in Japan set, the Kaiju faction unleashes colossal creatures to wreak havoc on the battlefield. This faction's strategy revolves around playing actions on bases to emerge victorious. With a complexity rating of Medium-High, the Kaiju demand a thoughtful approach. They are joined in this set by three other unique factions: Itty Critters, Magical Girls, and Mega Troopers.
cardsTeaser:
The Kaiju faction boasts a unique card composition, featuring just 3 minions and 17 actions, a significant departure from the typical 10 minions and 10 actions. Notably, they also have a titan in their ranks. With a total base power of 9, or an average of 3 per minion, their minions are relatively low in power compared to the standard 30 total power and 3 average power. However, the Kaiju's strength lies in their actions, many of which are power actions that directly contribute to the total power without relying on minions. When including these actions, the Kaiju's total power jumps to 34, with an average power of 2.83.

Their action lineup consists of 9 play-on-base actions, including The Folly of Men, Oh, No!, three instances of Radioactive Breath, two Stomps, Tail Smash, and Wade Through the Buildings. Additionally, they have 8 standard actions, one of which affects one or more minions, including two Kaiju Alliances, two Kaiju Conflicts, two Pick Up A Bus, There Goes Tokyo, and They Say He’s Got to Go. Notably, none of their actions directly increase a minion's power, and there are no play-on-minion actions in their arsenal.
actionList:
2x Kaiju Alliance - Reduce the breakpoint of all bases by 4 until the end of the turn.
2x Kaiju Conflict - Play two extra actions.
1x Oh, No! - power 2 - Play on a base. Play or move Gorgodzolla to here. Ongoing: You gain +2 power here.
2x Pick Up a Bus - Choose an action in your discard pile that can be played on a base and place it into your hand.
3x Radioactive Breath - power 3 - Play on a base. Destroy any number of minions here of power 2 or less. Ongoing: You gain +3 power here.
2x Stomp - power 2 - Play on a base. Reduce its breakpoint by 3 until the end of the turn. Ongoing: You gain +2 power here.
1x Tail Smash - power 4 - Play on a base. Destroy a minion you don’t control here of power 3 or less. Ongoing: You gain +4 power here.
1x The Folly of Men - power 2 - Play on a base. Ongoing: Your minions and other actions on this base cannot be destroyed by other players' cards. You gain +2 power here.
1x There Goes Tokyo - If Gorgodzolla is in play, destroy its base. Move Gorgodzolla to another base and replace the destroyed base normally.
1x They Say He’s Got to Go - Move a titan to another base.
1x Wade Through the Buildings - power 4 - Play on a base. Destroy all other players’ actions on this base. Ongoing: You gain +4 power here.
actions:
2x Kaiju Alliance - Reduce the breakpoint of all bases by 4 until the end of the turn.

2x Kaiju Conflict - Play two extra actions.

1x Oh, No! - power 2 - Play on a base. Play or move Gorgodzolla to here. Ongoing: You gain +2 power here.

2x Pick Up a Bus - Choose an action in your discard pile that can be played on a base and place it into your hand.

3x Radioactive Breath - power 3 - Play on a base. Destroy any number of minions here of power 2 or less. Ongoing: You gain +3 power here.

2x Stomp - power 2 - Play on a base. Reduce its breakpoint by 3 until the end of the turn. Ongoing: You gain +2 power here.

1x Tail Smash - power 4 - Play on a base. Destroy a minion you don’t control here of power 3 or less. Ongoing: You gain +4 power here.

1x The Folly of Men - power 2 - Play on a base. Ongoing: Your minions and other actions on this base cannot be destroyed by other players' cards. You gain +2 power here.

1x There Goes Tokyo - If Gorgodzolla is in play, destroy its base. Move Gorgodzolla to another base and replace the destroyed base normally.

1x They Say He’s Got to Go - Move a titan to another base.

1x Wade Through the Buildings - power 4 - Play on a base. Destroy all other players’ actions on this base. Ongoing: You gain +4 power here.
characters:
1x Kaijookey - power 4 - Ongoing: This minion has +1 power for each of your actions played on this base.

1x Tiny Priestesses - power 3 - Play or move Gorgodzolla to here.

1x Johnny - power 2 - You may return one of your actions on a base to your hand and play it here as an extra action.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Big in Japan rulebook:

Gorgodzolla: You may play these instead of a regular minion play whether or not you have any minions in hand. No restrictions on minion play apply to titan play.

Kaijookey, Wade through the Buildings, Gorgodzolla: Actions on minions do not count.

Oh No!, Tiny Priestesses: If this is used to play or move Gorgodzolla to their base, Gorgodzolla does NOT get a +1 power counter. (added by clarifications for "Tiny Priestesses" and "Oh No!" on https://smashup-rulebook.alderac.com/wiki/Kaiju)

There Goes Tokyo: Cards immune to destruction are discarded instead, because their base left play. (added by The Bigger Geekier Box)

They Say He’s Got to Go: This can move any player’s titan. (added by The Bigger Geekier Box)
suggestionTeaser: Kaiju teams up perfectly with factions that trigger effects when cards are played on bases or those that grant additional action plays.
synergy:
Kaiju pairs exceptionally well with factions that excel at playing actions on bases or generating additional actions. Factions that fit this bill can amplify the Kaiju's formidable abilities, leading to devastating combinations.

Steampunks, in particular, form a powerhouse partnership with Kaiju, offering unparalleled control over play-on-base actions, as well as enhanced power, mobility, and protection. It's worth noting that the action Change in Venue can be replayed on the base it originated from, a tactic that, while typically less useful for Steampunks, can still be leveraged to great effect.

Wizards also make a strong match, as they can increase the overall number of actions played, allowing Kaiju to maximize their impact. Meanwhile, Mythic Greeks can reap additional benefits from playing actions, thanks to their Argonauts being treated as play-on-base actions.

Tricksters bring a unique set of skills to the table, providing an abundance of play-on-base actions while also enabling invasions and opponent control on bases. Lastly, combining Kaiju with Dragons results in a doubly destructive force, capable of laying waste to bases with ease.
tips:
Unleash destruction with the Kaiju faction, but be prepared to play the long game. The imbalance between minions and actions, combined with Gorgodzolla's specific play requirements and power counter accumulation, means that Kaiju's strength builds gradually. Focus on dismantling your opponents' defenses by demolishing their minions and actions, and use defensive strategies to bide your time until you're ready to strike.

The Johnny card is a valuable asset, offering flexibility and cunning. Use it to replay an action on a different base or repeat an action on the same base, opening up opportunities for creative combinations. For example, deploy Radioactive Breath on multiple bases, unleash Stomp twice on a single base, or unleash a devastating Tail Smash, all made possible by Johnny's versatility.
effects:
In the Big in Japan expansion, the Kaiju faction is led by the formidable titan Gorgodzolla, a behemoth that embodies the faction's destructive power. Unlike other titans that simply support their faction's existing gameplay, Kaiju's cards bring Gorgodzolla to life, depicting its massive size and abilities as it stomps, lifts buses, and unleashes radioactive breath. This focus on titans sets Kaiju apart from other factions.

With a minimal roster of just three minions, Kaiju shifts the spotlight to Gorgodzolla, which effectively replaces traditional minions. The faction boasts nine play-on-base actions that generate power on their own, amplifying the destruction. Gorgodzolla's delayed entry onto a base, waiting for two actions to be played, mirrors the gradual devastation of bases over time. When Gorgodzolla finally joins the fray, it accelerates the chaos, earning +1 Power Counters and drawing additional cards to further wreak havoc.

name: Magical Girls
teaser: Use abilities that get better the more minions you have on the same base.
mechanics: Use abilities that get better the more minions you have on the same base.
description: Meet the Magical Girls, a faction from the Big in Japan set that excels at teamwork and synergy. By combining their unique abilities, they can tackle even the toughest challenges with ease. With a complexity rating of Low-Medium, they're a great fit for players of all skill levels. The Magical Girls are joined in this set by three other factions: Itty Critters, Kaiju, and Mega Troopers.
cardsTeaser:
The Magical Girls faction is composed of 10 minions and 10 actions, with a notable highlight being the inclusion of a titan. In terms of raw minion power, they pack a slightly lower punch, with a total base power of 28 and an average of 2.8 power per minion.

Their action lineup features a diverse range of effects, including one play-on-minion action, Magical Staff, which also has the ability to directly boost a minion's power. The standard actions are plentiful, with nine in total, and include four that impact one or more minions: Celestial Teleport appears twice, along with two instances of Coordination, as well as Coronet Attack, Kiss the Sky Spell, Lunar Healing Love Spell, Purge the Demon, and Silver Shard. Notably, there are no play-on-base actions among their options.
actionList:
2x Celestial Teleport - Play on one of your minions. Talent: Move this minion to another base.
2x Coordination - Play either an extra minion or Walking Castle.
1x Coronet Attack - Destroy a minion you don’t control with power less than or equal to the number of your minions at the same base.
1x Kiss the Sky Spell - Place a minion from your discard pile into your hand. You may play an extra action this turn.
1x Lunar Healing Love Spell - Choose a minion in each player’s discard pile and place it into its owner’s hand.
1x Magical Staff - Play on your minion. Ongoing: This minion gains +1 power. When this minion goes to the discard pile, place it on top of your deck instead.
1x Purge the Demon - Choose a card. Destroy an action on it, OR remove all counters on it.
1x Silver Shard - All players shuffle all minions in their discard piles into their decks.
actions:
2x Celestial Teleport - Play on one of your minions. Talent: Move this minion to another base.

2x Coordination - Play either an extra minion or Walking Castle.

1x Coronet Attack - Destroy a minion you don’t control with power less than or equal to the number of your minions at the same base.

1x Kiss the Sky Spell - Place a minion from your discard pile into your hand. You may play an extra action this turn.

1x Lunar Healing Love Spell - Choose a minion in each player’s discard pile and place it into its owner’s hand.

1x Magical Staff - Play on your minion. Ongoing: This minion gains +1 power. When this minion goes to the discard pile, place it on top of your deck instead.

1x Purge the Demon - Choose a card. Destroy an action on it, OR remove all counters on it.

1x Silver Shard - All players shuffle all minions in their discard piles into their decks.
characters:
1x Lunar Captain - power 5 - Talent: Choose a minion in your discard pile with power less than or equal to the number of your minions here, and place it into your hand.

1x Sakura Warrior - power 4 - Talent: Reduce a minion’s power here by the number of your minions here until the start of your next turn.

1x Technomagical Lass - power 4 - Talent: Destroy a minion you don’t control here with power less than or equal to the number of your minions here.

1x Bewitching Gal - power 3 - Talent: Reduce the breakpoint of this base by the number of your minions here until the end of the turn.

1x Fancy Suit Lad - power 3 - Ongoing: Your other minions here are not affected by other players’ cards.

1x Power Maid - power 3 - Talent: Choose a minion with power less than or equal to the number of your minions here, and move it to or from here.

1x Black Magicat - power 1 - Search your deck and/or discard pile for Lunar Captain and place it into your hand.

1x White Magicat - power 1 - Search your deck and/or discard pile for Power Maid and place it into your hand.

2x Rainbow Girl - power 2 - Your other minions here gain +1 power until the end of the turn.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Big in Japan rulebook:

Bewitching Gal, Sakura Warrior: The amount of reduction is calculated once, and does not change even if the number of minions there changes.

Black Magicat, White Magicat: It is now part of the rules that cards that tell you to search your deck also require that you reveal the card searched for, and shuffle your deck afterwards.

Purge the Demon: You may choose any card, including a base or a titan.

Walking Castle: You may play these instead of a regular minion play whether or not you have any minions in hand. No restrictions on minion play apply to titan play.

Rainbow Girl: This does not affect minions played after this card. (added by The Bigger Geekier Box)
suggestionTeaser: Magical Girls shine when paired with factions that rely heavily on minions, particularly those with initially weaker minions that can be boosted to greater power at crucial moments.
synergy:
Magical Girls thrive when paired with factions that focus on minions and those that gain power over time. Their abilities often rely on specific situations, making them a great complement to factions with minions that start weak but grow stronger.

Robots make an excellent match, as their emphasis on teamwork and replaying Mister Alpha synergizes perfectly with Magical Girl actions. Don't forget to utilize Tech Center to boost your Robot minions' power. 

Ghosts may seem like an unlikely pairing, but Magical Girls can help overcome the Ghosts' limited hand size. Lunar Captain can retrieve specific minions from the discard pile, mitigating the need to discard minions from your hand to activate Ghost abilities.

Shapeshifters also pair well with Magical Girls, as G.E.L.F. can fetch most Magical Girl minions, including Black Magicat. Since Mimics have a power of 0, Lunar Captain can recover them for free, adding to your minion count.

Mythic Horses and Magical Girls share a similar strategy, focusing on having numerous minions on bases. Magical Girls offer control and recursion, complementing Mythic Horses' power-increasing abilities. Coordination and Togetherness Power work well together, allowing for extra minion plays, while Adventure Power helps converge your minions onto a single base.

Superheroes can also benefit from Magical Girls, as Lunar Captain is a great target for Mild-Mannered Citizen. This allows you to play the same Citizen repeatedly, taking advantage of both factions' abilities to return minions from the discard pile to your hand or deck.

Itty Critters also make a good match, as Magicats and Rainbow Girls are ideal minions for Critter Champion and Critter Coaches to fetch. Magical Girls' "minion count" effects can be quickly amplified by the extra minion plays provided by Itty Critters. Magical Staff, Kiss the Sky Spell, and Lunar Healing Love Spell can retrieve Critter Champion and Critter Coach, while Silver Shard can shuffle power 2 minions back into the deck.
tips:
Mastering the Magical Girls faction requires a deep understanding of their unique strengths and weaknesses. While they may lack overall power and burst potential, their talents can be leveraged to great effect with careful planning and strategic play. To maximize their abilities, it's essential to balance the need to power up their talents with the risk of losing minions when they're gathered together.

To achieve success, focus on preserving key minions like Lunar Captain, Power Maid, and Sakura Warrior by using other minions to score on bases instead. Keeping Magicats in reserve can provide valuable flexibility when combined with Lunar Captain and Power Maid. Sakura Warrior's ability to reduce the power of your own minions can be a high-risk, high-reward strategy to keep your minions on bases until you can secure a decisive victory.

The Bait and Switch maneuver is a powerful tool for breaking bases, and the Magical Girls' mobility is a significant asset, thanks to cards like Walking Castle, Power Maid, and Celestial Teleport. With Power Maid's ability to move enemy minions and the protection offered by Walking Castle and Fancy Suit Lad, you can outmaneuver your opponents. To make the most of these abilities, target and disrupt other strong factions early on, creating space for your Magical Girls to operate and shuffle their minions effectively.
effects: In the Magical Girls faction, individual minions boast distinct abilities, yet they share a common thread - their powers surge when teamed up with other minions on the same base. Starting out as lone, relatively weak entities, they transform into formidable forces as their numbers grow and they work together. The faction's abilities often focus on base-hopping mobility and reclaiming discarded cards, with each Magical Girl bringing a unique skillset that bolsters the collective strength of the team. The Big in Japan set introduces the Magical Girls' titan, Walking Castle, to the fray.

name: Mega Troopers
teaser: Wait until a base scores to use all kinds of strong Special abilities.
mechanics: Wait until a base scores to use all kinds of strong Special abilities.
description: Meet the Mega Troopers, a faction from the Big in Japan expansion. This team of heroes excels at joining forces to achieve victory, often turning the tide of battle in their favor at the most critical moment. They are joined in this set by three other unique factions: the Itty Critters, the mighty Kaiju, and the enchanting Magical Girls.
cardsTeaser:
The Mega Troopers faction is composed of 10 minions and 10 actions, boasting a unique titan among their ranks. Their minions pack a total base power of 30, averaging 3 power per minion. However, the faction's true strength lies in its numerous ability-enhanced minions, which can significantly surpass their base power values.

Delving into their action cards, the Mega Troopers have a distinct lineup, featuring no play-on-minion or play-on-base actions. Instead, they rely on 10 standard actions, including four that manipulate one or more minions: **Blitzing Sword Attack**, **Form Megabot!** (available twice), **It’s Blitzin’ Time!** (also available twice), **Lightning Crystal**, **Lightning Rescue!**, **Mega Attack**, **Plan For More!**, and **Power Pose**. Notably, two of these actions - **It’s Blitzin’ Time!** (with two copies) - directly amplify a minion's power, further bolstering the faction's capabilities.
actionList:
1x Blitzing Sword Attack - Special: Before a base scores where you have a titan, if you are not winning there, destroy a minion there of power 4 or less.
2x Form Megabot! - Play or move Megabot to a base where you have at least two minions.
2x It’s Blitzin’ Time! - One of your minions gains +3 power until the end of the turn.
1x Lightning Crystal - Destroy an action on a base or on a minion.
1x Lightning Rescue! - Special: Before a base scores, play an action as a special action.
1x Mega Attack - Destroy a minion with power less than the total power of your minions at its base.
1x Plan For More! - Reveal the top three cards of your deck. Draw any minions revealed; you may play one of them as an extra minion. Return the rest in any order.
1x Power Pose - Special: After a base scores where you were the winner, draw two cards.
actions:
1x Blitzing Sword Attack - Special: Before a base scores where you have a titan, if you are not winning there, destroy a minion there of power 4 or less.

2x Form Megabot! - Play or move Megabot to a base where you have at least two minions.

2x It’s Blitzin’ Time! - One of your minions gains +3 power until the end of the turn.

1x Lightning Crystal - Destroy an action on a base or on a minion.

1x Lightning Rescue! - Special: Before a base scores, play an action as a special action.

1x Mega Attack - Destroy a minion with power less than the total power of your minions at its base.

1x Plan For More! - Reveal the top three cards of your deck. Draw any minions revealed; you may play one of them as an extra minion. Return the rest in any order.

1x Power Pose - Special: After a base scores where you were the winner, draw two cards.
characters:
1x Red Trooper - power 5 - Talent: Play or move Megabot to here.

1x Blue Trooper - power 4 - Special: Before this base scores, this minion gains +2 power until the end of the turn.

1x Yellow Trooper - power 4 - Special: Before this base scores, you may move another one of your minions to here.

1x Black Trooper - power 3 - Ongoing: After a special ability is used, this minion gains +1 power until the end of the turn.

1x Green Trooper - power 3 - Special: Before a base scores, you may play this minion there as an extra minion.

1x Pink Trooper - power 3 - Special: After this base scores, return one of your minions here of power 3 or less to your hand instead of the discard pile.

4x Beta 6 - power 2 - Special: Before this base scores, this minion gains +1 power until the end of the turn.
clarifications:
Here are the official clarifications as they appear in the Big in Japan rulebook:

Black Trooper: This triggers each time any player uses a special ability, and can trigger multiple times in a turn.

Megabot: You may play these instead of a regular minion play whether or not you have any minions in hand. No restrictions on minion play apply to titan play.
suggestionTeaser: Mega Troopers have a unique limitation, restricting them from summoning additional minions, despite reaping benefits when multiple are stationed on a single base.
synergy: Mega Troopers thrive on bases crowded with their own minions, despite being unable to play additional ones. To maximize their potential, pair them with factions that can provide substantial support. Factions with special abilities are particularly well-suited to complement the Mega Troopers, amplifying their effectiveness in battle.
tips:
Mega Troopers may seem straightforward at first, but their unique interactions and abilities can lead to surprising outcomes. The faction thrives when its minions work together, with half of the Troopers relying on the presence of other minions to maximize their potential. Megabot, in particular, requires a minion presence and amplifies their power, making teamwork a key strategy.

The Black Trooper's ability to gain +1 power from any source, including enemies and allies, adds a layer of complexity to the faction's gameplay. Mega Troopers boast an impressive array of special abilities, rivaling any other faction in the game. Many of these abilities trigger when a base is broken or scored, similar to those found in other factions like Shinobi, Scout, Pirate King, and Cthulhu's Chosen, and are featured on cards like Green Trooper, Pink Trooper, Megabot, and Blue Trooper, as well as Beta 6.

These abilities often require the relevant card to be in play, reducing the element of surprise but allowing for careful planning. As a result, Mega Troopers excel at turning the tide of battle in their favor at the last moment, creating an air of suspense and uncertainty around base breaks. Like Ninjas, they prefer bases to break when all players have contributed roughly equally, making it difficult for opponents to anticipate their moves.

A cautious opponent will keep track of the Mega Troopers' threat level on a base and may avoid engaging, delaying the base's break. While other factions may be more aggressive in breaking bases, Mega Troopers have ways to unleash a burst of power and take control. Cards like Green Trooper and Lightning Rescue! can be particularly unpredictable, and combining Megabot with Blitzing Sword Attack can lead to a stunning reversal of fortune. By carefully timing these cards, players can flip a losing situation into a winning one.
effects:
The Mega Troopers faction excels at turning the tide of battle with their special abilities, which kick in just before a base scores. At first glance, these abilities may seem unassuming, but they pack a significant punch when the stakes are high. 

As part of the Big in Japan expansion, the Mega Troopers are joined by the formidable Megabot, a titan that bolsters their ranks.
